pages.raw_text:
Police Commissioner Tuerke left
last night for trip of inspection
among the police departments of
the east.


Alleging prejudice on the part Of
Judge J. d. Hinkle and the city's
prosecuting counsel, E. E. Sargent,
S. T. Crane, the attorney found
guilty of disorderly conduct in the
street speaking fight. has asked for
a new trial


T. e. Crane, charged with prac.
ticing medicine without a license,
yesterday forfeited his bond of $3.50
in Judge Kennan's court.


The two sons of ]. p. M. Rich
ards, the banker. Joe and J. ",
have opened offices as financial
agents" in the Exchange bank build
ing.


The Occur d'AIene farmers fight
ing the power dam of the w. w. p.
at Post Falls charge that much of
the malaria and typhoid of that To-
cality IS due to stagnant water caus-
ed by the dam.


In an interview at Portland, Ore,
yesterday, Mrs. i. w. Hutton of
Spokane. now en- her way to lower
California. said that militant
movement to secure equal suffrage
for American women would not be
necessary as in England, and that
suffrage is coming soon through
more peaceaDle means.


Bruce CIerdennine has borrowed
100000 for five years from the Spo-
kane C Eastern Trust Co. at SIC
per cent on the Dexter hotel prop
erty, Second and Wall. This will
refund a mortgage of S25 900 nego-
tiated two years ago at per cent.


James r. Walsh, right Of way
man for the Milwaukee, is in the
city.


John T. Burns, secretary of the
next Dry Farm congress. to be held
here October to 6. has opened
